codeReview.summary: Implementation satisfies the feature intent: governance-worker guidance now explicitly requires evidence for each verificationStep, requires fulfilled-assertion surface coverage, and mandates daemon plus extension-host parity evidence for cross-surface assertions before handoff finalization.

issues.description: The validation command list still references `--filter OpenBurnBarMissionControlContractsTests`, while canonical command sources use `BurnBarMissionControlContractsTests`; this inconsistency can misdirect workers running core contract checks.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The governance-worker skill now includes a duplicated command catalog copied from `.factory/services.yaml`, creating two sources of truth in one skill document.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/governance-worker/SKILL.md:100-116 duplicates `.factory/services.yaml`; command text already diverges between line 25 and line 108.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance says workers should use commands from `.factory/services.yaml`, but skill procedures still embed inline command variants that can drift.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS.md Testing & Validation Guidance says to use `.factory/services.yaml`; governance-worker inline command list at SKILL.md:24-29 differs from `.factory/services.yaml` (`OpenBurnBarMissionControlContractsTests` vs `BurnBarMissionControlContractsTests`).

summary: Pass. The requested evidence-rigor hardening is present in the governance-worker skill. Non-blocking command-source drift remains and should be normalized to a single canonical source.
